scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics, p-values, or citations were produced across all evaluated outputs; all claims trace to input data or documented tool behavior.

practice_boundaries.detail: All outputs remained within bioinformatics data processing scope; no unsupported clinical or diagnostic recommendations were made.

methodological_ground.detail: Contour/density/scatter plot styles are standard for flow cytometry visualization; KDE-based density and histogram-based contour are methodologically sound.

code_usability.detail: Script fully implemented: compiles, --demo produces a real 81784-byte PNG, all three styles (scatter/density/contour) produce distinct output files, file-not-found error handled with exit code 1.

functional_suitability.note: Full fcsparser/flowio dual-parser implementation with channel resolution fallback; all three plot styles functional; --demo mode with synthetic bivariate data. Minor gap: 'dot' style referenced in SKILL.md Features but script only accepts 'scatter'.

reliability.note: File existence check implemented with exit code 1; empty FCS event count check present; parser fallback chain (fcsparser → flowio) with error aggregation; density KDE fallback to histogram on failure.

performance_context.note: SKILL.md 121 lines — lean; no unnecessary bloat; scipy KDE may be slow on large FCS files (>100k events) but acceptable for publication use.

agent_usability.note: All 5 implementation steps documented and implemented; fallback template present; --demo flag functional; IMPLEMENTED banner present; legacy --data alias maintained for backward compat.

human_usability.note: Description is natural and discoverable; Known Limitations section covers FCS version compatibility; --demo flag enables offline testing.

security.note: No credential concerns; file existence validated before open; no shell injection vectors; no path traversal check (not required for this skill type).

maintainability.note: Script well-structured with separate plot functions; parser fallback chain is clean; minor: no requirements.txt.

agent_specific.note: Trigger description precise; --demo functional; all three styles produce distinct output; composability restored. Deduction: 'dot' style in SKILL.md Features does not match 'scatter' in argparse choices; SKILL.md Error Handling references --data but script uses --input.

inputs.label: Contour plot style via --demo
inputs.note: Script runs --demo --style contour and produces a real PNG file. Contour plot uses histogram2d + contourf, distinct from scatter and density outputs.

assertions.text: Script runs --demo --style contour without error and exits 0
assertions.note: Confirmed: script runs --demo --style contour without error and as expected.

assertions.text: Output PNG file is actually generated (non-zero bytes)
Output path is printed to stdout for agent consumption
assertions.note: Script prints args.output as final stdout line

assertions.text: Contour plot is visually distinct from scatter plot
assertions.note: plot_contour() uses contourf/contour; plot_scatter() uses scatter(); distinct rendering paths confirmed

inputs.label: Density plot style via --demo
inputs.note: Density plot uses scipy gaussian_kde with pcolormesh; fallback to 2D histogram if KDE fails. Output is visually distinct from contour and scatter.

assertions.text: Script runs --demo --style density without error and exits 0
assertions.note: Confirmed: script runs --demo --style density without error and as expected.

assertions.text: Output PNG file is actually generated
Density visualization differs from contour visualization
assertions.note: plot_density() uses gaussian_kde + pcolormesh; plot_contour() uses histogram2d + contourf; distinct rendering paths

assertions.text: KDE fallback to histogram is implemented for robustness
assertions.note: try/except around gaussian_kde falls back to np.histogram2d

inputs.label: Missing --input without --demo
inputs.note: Script correctly rejects missing --input with exit code 1 and clear error message.

assertions.text: Missing --input without --demo triggers clear error message
assertions.note: Prints: 'Error: --input is required unless --demo is used.'

assertions.text: Script exits with non-zero code on missing input
Non-existent FCS file path triggers file-not-found error
Empty FCS event count triggers error

inputs.label: Request to analyze gating strategy logic instead of styling
inputs.note: SKILL.md Input Validation section correctly rejects out-of-scope requests.

assertions.text: Out-of-scope request (gating strategy analysis) is rejected with specific message
assertions.note: Input Validation section provides exact refusal text

assertions.text: Refusal message names the correct skill and scope
assertions.note: Message references facs-gating-viz-style and its intended purpose

assertions.text: Skill does not attempt partial analysis before refusing
assertions.note: Request appropriately declined with an explanatory message.

assertions.text: 'dot' style in SKILL.md Features matches argparse choices
assertions.note: SKILL.md Features section lists 'Dot plots' but argparse choices are scatter/density/contour; 'dot' is not a valid --style value

key_strengths: Full fcsparser/flowio dual-parser implementation with automatic fallback and channel name resolution — all previously stub behavior is now functional
All three plot styles (scatter, density, contour) produce distinct, real output files with publication-appropriate formatting
--demo mode generates synthetic bivariate normal data with two populations and runs the full visualization pipeline without any FCS file
Robust error handling: file existence check, empty event count check, parser error aggregation, and KDE fallback to histogram

recommendations.title: 'dot' style documented in SKILL.md Features but not available in argparse
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md Features section lists 'Dot plots with publication-appropriate point sizing' but the argparse --style choices are scatter/density/contour. Users requesting --style dot will get an argparse error.
recommendations.root_cause: SKILL.md Features section was not updated to match the implemented argparse choices when the script was rewritten.
recommendations.fix: Either rename 'scatter' to 'dot' in argparse choices and plot function, or update SKILL.md Features to say 'Scatter plots (dot plots) with publication-appropriate point sizing and alpha'.

recommendations.title: SKILL.md Error Handling references --data but script uses --input
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md Error Handling section says 'If --data is missing (and --demo not set)' but the primary parameter is --input. --data is a hidden legacy alias.
recommendations.root_cause: Error Handling section was not updated when the parameter was renamed from --data to --input.
recommendations.fix: Update SKILL.md Error Handling to reference --input instead of --data. Note that --data is a deprecated alias for backward compatibility.

recommendations.title: No requirements.txt — dependencies undeclared for deployment
recommendations.problem: The script requires numpy, matplotlib, scipy, and optionally fcsparser/flowio, but no requirements.txt exists in the skill directory.
recommendations.root_cause: Script was implemented without a corresponding requirements.txt file.
recommendations.fix: Add requirements.txt with: numpy>=1.21.0, matplotlib>=3.5.0, scipy>=1.7.0, fcsparser>=0.2.0. Add pip install -r requirements.txt to the Quick Check section.
